texto: His head pounded with such intensity, he feared it would explode. It was hard for him to control himself around her. It was in his nature to crave her blood, and the craving was like a toothache that wouldn't stop throbbing.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Angela Snyder: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “His head pounded with such intensity, he feared it would explode. It was hard for him to control …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, romance-novels, vampire-romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de vampire-romance.

frase_original: His head pounded with such intensity, he feared it would explode. It was hard for him to control himself around her. It was in his nature to crave her blood, and the craving was like a toothache that wouldn't stop throbbing.
